prompt: Can you reword the introduction paragraph in my architecture doc? It's a bit wordy and I want it to be punchier. Here's what's there now: 'Our system is composed of several interconnected components that work together in a coordinated fashion to deliver the desired functionality.'

assertions.name: Applies Hemingway principles
assertions.description: Output is concise, cuts unnecessary words (e.g., 'composed of', 'interconnected', 'work together in a coordinated fashion')

assertions.name: More impactful and direct
assertions.description: Reworded version is punchier and more specific than the original

assertions.name: Preserves core meaning
assertions.description: Maintains the essential information about system components and functionality

assertions.name: Suitable for architecture doc intro
assertions.description: Tone and style fit an architecture document's opening
